raw_response: To add to my agreement with Option C (0.80 ± 0.35mm), this range allows for slight variations based on patient anatomy without risking excessive contact which could compromise the integrity of the restoration or cause discomfort. A wider gap would unnecessarily increase material usage and cost, whereas too narrow a gap increases the likelihood of issues down the line due to misalignment or plaque accumulation. Therefore, maintaining an optimal middle ground represented by Option C ensures both functionality and safety. My answer remains C.
